meta_title: For Students: passive income to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to passive income and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Passive Income to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to build passive income streams and overcome the 'no portfolio' barrier. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for students.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is solved by shipping a micro-SaaS that generates passive income. By automating a specific utility tool using Antigravity and Claude Code, you demonstrate technical competency while building a real-world asset. This dual-value approach transforms your GitHub from a graveyard of tutorials into a revenue-generating proof of skill.
content.intro:
You stare at a blank GitHub profile, the silence of your commit history echoing the anxiety of every job application rejection. The industry demands experience you haven't been given the chance to earn, and the 'no portfolio' wall feels insurmountable. You aren't just missing a job; you are missing a signal that you can deliver value.

But the rules have changed. You don't need a senior role to build a digital asset. By deploying a passive income-generating application, you shift the narrative from 'I am a student looking for work' to 'I am a developer who builds products that solve problems.' This is how you bridge the gap between theory and reality.

steps.title: Define the Micro-Utility
steps.description: Identify a small, repetitive task in a niche market that can be automated. We will build a simple SaaS that solves this.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a senior SaaS architect. Identify a high-demand micro-utility tool for productivity that can be built in a weekend and monetized via subscription.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Research 3 micro-SaaS ideas in the productivity niche with low barrier to entry and high recurring value.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Infrastructure
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a production-ready boilerplate to ensure your architecture is industry-standard from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, scalable Next.js and Supabase boilerplate for a subscription-based micro-SaaS, including Stripe integration and user authentication.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize a Next.js project with Tailwind CSS and Supabase auth as structured by the Antigravity design pattern.'

steps.title: Implement Core Logic
steps.description: Build the primary feature that delivers value. Focus on a single, flawless function that justifies the subscription cost.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the core business logic for a document-summarization utility, ensuring it is modular, error-handled, and optimized for low-latency.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create the API route for the document summarizer using the logic defined in the Antigravity spec.'

steps.title: Integrate Payments
steps.description: Connect Stripe to your app so you can actually collect revenue, proving your ability to integrate third-party APIs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure Stripe webhook handler to manage subscription status and user access levels within our database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure Stripe checkout session and webhook endpoint for user subscription management.'

steps.title: Deploy and Portfolio-ize
steps.description: Deploy to Vercel and document the process. Your README should explain the business logic and the technical stack used.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a professional README.md that highlights the problem, the technical architecture, and the revenue model for this project.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Generate a professional README.md file that emphasizes the system design and business impact of this SaaS.'

pro_tips: Always focus on 'Time to First Dollar'—if a feature doesn't directly contribute to the user paying you, cut it from the MVP.
Structure your README like a product case study, not a list of commands. Hiring managers want to see business logic, not just code.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your checkout flow; a broken payment gateway is the fastest way to lose credibility.
Automate your deployment pipeline. Showing you understand CI/CD workflows is more impressive than the app itself.
Don't aim for perfection; aim for 'shippable.' A deployed app with 1 user is infinitely more valuable than a 'perfect' app that never leaves your local machine.
content.next_steps: Once deployed, share your project on IndieHackers and Twitter/X. Engage with the community, document the feedback you receive, and iterate on the product. This loop is the foundation of a real engineering career.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are a builder. By transforming your technical skills into a revenue-generating asset, you separate yourself from 99% of the competition. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
